comments.author: @karateka3851
comments.text:
"@Nganga-w2o
16 hours ago
What do you mean by discovered by the Portuguese?
Where there no people before the y arrived?"

Were you paying attention?!

He explained it clearly.

When the Portuguese arrived in 1456 they found no one on the archipelago.

Absolutely no traces of human presence, no graves, no human burials, no artifacts, to sum up NOTHING.

They brought captive slaves from Africa, as Cabo Verde was an obligatory stopping point in the Atlantic for slave traders on their way to the Americas.

Portuguese were the first inhabitants, later they brought in slaves, they mixed HEAVILY.

A new people was born.

These facts are not open for debate.

All Historians and  Anthropologists agree on them.

UNESCO too.

Cheers.

comments.author: @puntacanamike
comments.text: I get to experience those Cape Verde storms here in the Dominican Republic. My introduction to the country was Cesaria Evora many years ago. One day my feet will touch Cabo Verde.
